preamble: The voiceless glottal fricative /h/ can undergo palatalization to the voiceless alveolo-palatal fricative /ɕ/ in certain phonetic environments. This sound change involves a shift in the place of articulation from the glottis to the alveolo-palatal region, typically triggered by the presence of a following front vowel.
phoneticEffects: Palatalization, change in place of articulation (glottal to alveolo-palatal)

examples.note: In Asante Twi, the /h/ in words like 'hia' (to meet, from Bono dialect) palatalizes to /ɕ/ before the high front vowel /i/, resulting in 'hyia'. The 'hy' orthography represents the /ɕ/ sound.

sources: Adomako, K. (2018). Underapplication in an Akan language game. Nordic Journal of African Studies, 27(4), 1-25.
Abakah, E. N. (2016). Hypotheses on the Diachronic Development of the Akan Language Group. J. Univers. Lang., 17(1), 1-51.
